[PDF_PAGE 180 | score=8] 454—2 preferred study to action and disliked leaving the Tower of High Sorcery at Wayreth, he re- fused to retire while Raistlin remained a threat. He feared his decision to let Raistlin take his Test at an early age propelled him toward Evil. n the Fabulous finded and Nosy. A lovable old curmudgeon, Fizban was actu- ally an avatar of the god Paladine. In the Fourth Age, this bumbling, grandfatherly wiz- ard often appeared at just the right moment to deliver much-needed magical aid—or chicken feathers, if his spell didn’t work. Tasslehoff was always helping him find his lost hat. а D Justarius Thoughtful and Contemplative. The head of the Order of Red Robes, Justarius became leader of the Wi
[PDF_PAGE 179 | score=6] XN is v << = Soy = 27 LEG 7 A «У >) < mained fanatically loyal to the Blue Lady until his death. This handsome but dark man's wild nature gave him berserker fury in battle. His quick temper and lust for pleasure once led him to kill the son of a noble in a fight over a woman—and led to his death when he as- saulted Laurana during the War of the Lance. Suit of Helms ‘Tough heroes and villains with a great deal of stamina appear in the Suit of Helms. 1 Ф Caramon Majere Thorough and Brave. Caramon, the burly, good-hearted Hero of the Lance, has always been slow to act, preferring to think a matter over carefully before deciding on a course of action. After the war, he retired from. adventuring 
[PDF_PAGE 142 | score=4] 530-522рс: Kernen Skirmishes. Ogres from the new Khalkist nation of Kernen threaten trade routes, but Thoradin, Istar, and the Knights of Solamnia drive them back. Thoradin signs the Swordsheath Scroll, as well as the Dwarfmeld: an exclusive trade treaty with Istar. 490-476»c: Raiders/Greatmeld. Solamnia becomes more dependent on Istaran trade, currency, and ideals. Mean- while, barbarians from Khur, Nordmaar, of offenses on his list of Evil acts—the Proclamation of Manifest Virtue—face confiscation of their lands and prop- erty, slavery, execution, or the gladiato- rial arena. Priests of Istar begin losing their clerical magic as they drift away from the teachings of their gods and become m
[PDF_PAGE 143 | score=3] Signs of the Cataclysm In the days before the Great Rending, the gods sent thirteen omens to warn the folk of Ansalon of their approaching doom. How quickly the people forgot the prophesy foretold by the elf priest Loralon: “If ever man, in pride, should chal- lenge the gods, woe betide the world.” ‘These signs passed all but unrecognized among the populace: 1. The gods shall withdraw their hands from the world, and man shall face his doom alone. n. The sky shall lament and beat the earth with its tears and cries of anguish. ш. Fear shall visit the land. ту, Light shall be devoured. Hope shall flee. у. Darkness and despair shall be rekindled. м. The flame shall fail on the hearth, уп. The pl
[PDF_PAGE 145 | score=3] the forces of Good systematically hunt them down and work to destroy them. 355Ac: The Knighthood Evolves. Lord Gunthar uth Wistan is named the first Grand Master of the Knights of Solamnia since the Cataclysm. He orders an update of the Measure of the Knighthood for modern times. 356Ac: Master of Past and Present. Raistlin Majere and his brother Cara- mon use a magical device to journey into the past. They reach corrupted Istar, intent on changing history and challenging the Dark Queen. Raistlin becomes one with the spirit of the infa- mous wizard Fistandantilus. He moves through time and into the Abyss, hop- ing to defeat the Dark Queen. 357Ac: The Blue Lady's War. The Blue Lady, one of the
[PDF_PAGE 147 | score=3] EE N Reds N Wy 7 ATA SS] BARES X KS = [^5 V TAN A? KEES GA OM Sool i С 4sc: The Dragon Purge Begins Using a frightening arcane ritual, Malys begins to destroy the dragons of Ansa- lon, consuming their life energy and becoming more powerful with every victory. The lands around her new home, now known as the Desolation, become burnt wastes littered with the bones of slain dragons. Other Evil wyrms learn her secrets and begin destroying Good and Evil dragons alike in their desire for personal power. Human lands become mere battlefields for these titanic clashes. Most metallic dragons vanish to the Dragon Isles. Khellendros the Blue, the famed Skie of the War of the Lance and the Blue Lady's War
[PDF_PAGE 20 | score=2] ity codes two grades from "A" to “С” (ог "B" to “D”) to earn the additional two points of Strength. He also could reduce two codes by one grade each. Players may not elevate a character's ability codes to meet the minimum racial requirements. If a hero's codes do not qualify him for a specific race at this time, the player may not select it— after all, nonhuman heroes should be fairly uncommon, even in Krynn. If a hero's ability scores or codes are too high to qualify him for his desired race, a player can reduce them volun- tarily to the listed maximum. He may not increase other scores or codes as a result, though—reduced points are lost. Step Eight: Role Adopting a character archetype, suc
[PDF_PAGE 73 | score=2] AIS, A À AES ] =. = ر T» د 22 N Sesh ^, Al X P ^ AD. ^ o el 3 Ly Э ow many ‘spells do you have?” Steel asked, shout- ing over the rush of wind in their ears. “Are they powerful? What do they do?” “I may not speak of such matters,” Palin answered, clutching the spellbook under one arm. “It is forbidden.” Steel looked back at him, sud- denly grinned. “The hell it is. You don't have that many, do you?” Palin smiled. “They are very complex. And I didn't have much time to study them." “How many do you have?” “One. But,” Palin added gravely, “it’s a good one.” —Flying to do battle with Chaos, Dragons of Summer Flame Krynn’s history tells the story of the battle of heroism and virtue against darkne
